title: Snapshot

action.name: Deploy energy-efficient and solar-powered street lighting across all neighborhoods

ranking.explanation: Residential buildings account for 97% of the city's inventory, so this action has a strong impact driver. It is backed by the Plan de Mitigación Sector Energía, matches both air quality and mobility, and fits the city's medium timeframe preference, but it does not match the city's selected priority sector. Financial feasibility is a caveat because capacity is the constraint, not money, and technical assistance is needed.

limitations: Comparable project evidence is not available in the supplied context.
City-level quantified emissions reductions are not available for this action in the supplied context.
unsupported_claims: Do not state comparable project counts.

title: The Action

action.name: Deploy energy-efficient and solar-powered street lighting across all neighborhoods
action.description: The city will install LED-based (or other low emission technologies), solar-powered streetlights in all neighborhoods, including rural and peri-urban areas, to enhance safety, accessibility, and social inclusion while reducing energy costs and environmental impacts. Where feasible, the action prioritizes solar power generation, especially in areas with limited grid coverage or high carbon intensity. In locations where the electricity grid is already clean, the focus will be on maximizing lighting efficiency through LED retrofits and smart controls. Municipal roles include procuring equipment, establishing public–private financing mechanisms, and contracting long-term maintenance services to ensure system reliability. This action delivers strong co-benefits for communities of all sizes but requires careful planning to address maintenance and operational challenges.
action.intervention_summary: The city installs energy-efficient and solar-powered streetlights.
action.outcome_summary: Street lighting shifts to efficient LED and solar-powered fixtures.

limitations: Detailed local implementation scope is not available in the supplied context.

title: Policy Backing

action.name: Deploy energy-efficient and solar-powered street lighting across all neighborhoods

policy_evidence.evidence_text:
Impulsar el desarrollo de líneas y modelos de
financiamiento para reducir el uso de fuentes fósiles
mediante la electrificación de consumos.

El Plan establece 13 medidas clave que deberán implementarse durante el período 2025-2030, bajo la coordinación del Ministerio de Energía y con el apoyo de los actores colaboradores previamente identificados.
el Ministerio es reconocido como habilitante para el desarrollo del sector construcción
Responsable de transversalizar el enfoque de género y de derechos humanos en el diseño, formulación, implementación y seguimiento

los ministerios coadyuvantes corresponden al Ministerio de Obras Públicas, Ministerio de Vivienda y Urbanismo, Ministerio de Energía, Ministerio de Defensa (DIRECTEMAR), Ministerio del Interior y Seguridad Pública (SENAPRED) y Ministerio de Economía, Fomento y Turismo y Ministerio del Trabajo.

title: Legal Mandate & Delivery

action.name: Deploy energy-efficient and solar-powered street lighting across all neighborhoods

legal.ownership_description: Municipality has explicit legal authority to act directly.
legal.restrictions_description: No legal restrictions; no additional authorization required.
legal.legal_justification: La Ley 18.695 art. 3° letra d) establece el alumbrado público como función privativa municipal — es una de las pocas funciones exclusivas del municipio establecidas expresamente en la LOCM. El DL 3.063 (Rentas Municipales) art. 6° y siguientes regulan el financiamiento del alumbrado público. La Ley 21.305 promueve la eficiencia energética en alumbrado público como componente del Plan Nacional de EE. El municipio tiene competencia directa, exclusiva y plena — no requiere autorización de ningún otro nivel de gobierno. La coordinación con la empresa distribuidora es técnica, no decisional. Titularidad 100: función privativa municipal. Restricciones 100: sin restricciones jurídicas — plena autonomía. Alineación 100: plena con Ley 21.305 y Plan Nacional de EE.

limitations: Permit and SEIA applicability are not available in the supplied legal context.
unsupported_claims: Do not soften a blocked legal verdict.
